run_summary.summary: Two-day pipeline recap: Big new move — Liz submitted her SOJOS Sunglasses IG Reel + caption to Ruby today (Apr 22), so that deal moves to Approval, waiting on brand feedback. Aleve V/O revision is also confirmed this morning — Liz admitted she mixed up scripts and is redoing the VO today (both script and #AlevePartner disclosure needed). JLab March payment ($2,000 due 4/20) is still outstanding — manager is on it with accounting. Flexsteel on track: Steinhafels store visit locked for Thu Apr 23, Popfly issue resolved. Linkind concept resolved and Liz is ready to film. PDM sent several FIRM OFFER roster blasts today — Gaia Herbs flagged as best potential fit.

suggestions.message: PDM sent a FIRM OFFER today for Gaia Herbs — wellness/herbal brand, 1x IG Reel (45-60 sec) + 3-frame IG Story, budget $1,000–$5,000, timeline May–June 2026. Must submit via PDM Google Form using PDM-provided email. Could be a fit if Liz's audience skews wellness/natural living. Apply via PDM if interested.

PDM also sent a FIRM OFFER for a Ted Danson Anti-Aging campaign (built on a prior video with Jane Fonda that hit 15M+ views). Targeting lifestyle creators 50+, TikTok + IG Reels, timing end of May–June. Worth a look if Liz has reach in that demo.

Two more PDM FIRM OFFERs: Outdoorsy RV Rental (trade deal, not cash) and a Wine Brand (location-restricted: IN/TX/FL/GA). Both likely not fits — flagging FYI in case Liz wants to check the PDM morning emails.

deals.next_action: Check in on content approval with Ruby at SOJOS
deals.notes: [Apr 22]: Liz submitted IG Reel video + caption to Ruby for review via Google Drive. Caption opened with 'The sunnies I'm...' — awaiting brand feedback. Contract was signed Apr 17 (Ruby had updated it, Liz signed same day). Ruby sent IG Reel filming requirements Apr 20 (total length specs, sunglasses requirements). Content turned around quickly — 5 days from contract to submission.
deals.activity_note: Apr 22: Liz submitted IG Reel + caption to Ruby for brand review — moved to Approval

deals.next_action: Redo V/O with approved script language and add #AlevePartner disclosure (first 5 sec required by brief), then resubmit to brand
deals.notes: [Apr 22]: Liz confirmed she'll redo V/O today — admitted she had a brain lapse and mixed up scripts ('confused with another collab'). Brand raised two issues: (1) V/O didn't match the approved language in the brief, (2) all disclosures missing including #AlevePartner which must appear in the first 5 seconds. Approved script in Google Doc shared by manager: docs.google.com/d/1Y9GTFoO9pVS5c9oarq00onyFvIv_hXb4. Liz was unaware the brand had commented back with feedback (it was in the thread from Apr 13).
deals.activity_note: Apr 22: Aleve revision needed — V/O didn't match approved script and #AlevePartner disclosure missing. Liz redoing VO today.

deals.next_action: Follow up on JLab March campaign payment ($2,000 due 4/20/26 — overdue)
deals.notes: [Apr 21]: JLab March campaign payment ($2,000, due 4/20/26) still not received as of Apr 21. Manager in touch with JLab accounting — noted that other creators on the same campaign were paid ~2 weeks ago. Unclear why Liz's is delayed. Manager expected an update by Apr 22.
deals.activity_note: Apr 21: JLab March payment ($2k, due 4/20) still outstanding. Manager following up with JLab accounting — others on campaign paid 2 weeks ago.

deals.next_action: Film Reel 1 (Pre-Search concept ready) and submit draft via Popfly for review
deals.notes: [Apr 21]: Kenny confirmed Steinhafels visit Thu Apr 23, 11:30–12pm (for Reel 2 shopping footage). Plan: film Reel 1 this week, Reel 2 after store visit, Reels posting 1 week apart. Reel 3 (father-in-law reveal) after parents return from Florida — flying home May 12, filming May 13/14. Contract deadline for post 3 is end of May. Popfly platform access issue (managerial permissions request was a bug) resolved by Ian on Apr 21 — Kenny should re-try applying in Popfly.
deals.activity_note: Apr 21: Steinhafels visit locked in for Thu Apr 23. Filming schedule set: Reel 1 this week, Reel 2 post-store-visit, Reel 3 mid-May. Popfly access issue fixed.

deals.next_action: Film Linkind SP6 Reel with lights as main focus (50%+ screen time), meeting brand's talking points, and submit via manager for review
deals.notes: [Apr 21]: Manager gave green light to film and submit for brand review without waiting for formal concept approval. Brand's main concern: product must be front and center, talking points must be met. Liz plans: top 3 tips format, #1 is spring porch lighting (lights take 50%+ of video), will show assembly + app + day/night footage. Product arrived Apr 20 — looks great per Liz. Note: if this deal is stored as 'Smart Solar Path' in the tracker, this update may not match — please check.
deals.activity_note: Apr 21: Lights arrived (Apr 20). Concept back-and-forth resolved — manager says proceed to film and submit for brand review.
